match.userMessage:
9-panel cinematic storyboard sheet derived from Image 1. Mix Wide, Medium, and Tight shots of the same scene. No borders between panels.

Layout: 3x3 grid (9 panels) in a 16:9 landscape image.

Scene: Anamorphic film still, SCARLETT in a casual linen shirt with a small microphone clipped to the collar, leaning against a large sun-bleached rock with her body angled slightly toward the sea, head turned three-quarters, lips parted mid-sentence, one hand resting on the rock and the other loosely holding the microphone, tide gently rising around the base of the rocks in the background, wet sand and shallow foam visible, soft ocean horizon line beyond, natural late-afternoon coastal light with warm directional sunlight catching the edges of the rocks and creating gentle highlights on the linen fabric, shallow depth of field with the mid-ground figure in crisp focus and background surf softly blurred, locked eye-level frame on anamorphic lens with subtle lens flare from the sun low over the water, bright natural daylight with clean directional shadows and warm golden highlights, high-key airy color palette dominated by pale rock tones, ocean blues, and soft linen beige, cinematic social-first editorial style with tactile realism and effortless cool mood.

Match Image 1 exactly: same character(s) (face, hair, skin, clothing), lighting, color grade, and texture. If character reference sheets are provided, use them for likeness — do not render them as separate panels.

No text, dialogue bubbles, scene numbers, or watermarks.


  <reference-images>
    - Image 1 [PRIMARY SOURCE]: Primary source scene — generate 9 variant shots from this image
    - Image 2 [CHARACTER REF]: SCARLETT - Match the real-world appearance of Sienna Blake exactly
    - Image 3 [LOCATION REF]: EXT. BONDI BEACH - ROCKS - Sun-bleached rocks with rising tide behind
    
    IMPORTANT: Character, location, and element reference images are for IDENTITY CONSISTENCY ONLY. Do NOT reproduce them as separate panels or subjects. Elements (logos, products) must render faithfully when referenced but should appear naturally within the scene. All output panels must depict the scene from the PRIMARY SOURCE image.
  </reference-images>
